summary: German philosopher and economist who wrote Das Kapital and The Communist Manifesto (with Engels). Known for historical materialism, the labor theory of value (use-value vs. exchange-value, surplus value), the base-superstructure model, commodity fetishism, the theory of alienation, and the Theses on Feuerbach. Louis Althusser and Jacques Derrida are key interpreters.

works.name: Das Kapital and Political Economy
works.description: Das Kapital (Capital), subtitled 'A Critique of Political Economy,' is Marx's magnum opus, left unfinished. It distinguishes between 'use-value' (tied to physical properties) and 'exchange-value' (a measure of 'congealed labour-time'), theorizes that surplus value arises from the exploitation of labor by capital, and introduces commodity fetishism (attributing value to objects rather than their production labor). Marx called Bentham a leather-tongued 'arch-philistine.' The reserve army of labor (unemployed) depresses wages. G.A. Cohen defended Marx's theory of history in Karl Marx's Theory of History, founding the analytical Marxism / September Group.

clues.clue: Distinguishes use-value from exchange-value; surplus value arises from exploitation of labor
examples: distinguished between 'use-value,' which is tied to the 'physical properties of the commodity,' unlike its 'exchange-value'
argued that, in the capitalist mode of production, workers are alienated from the products of their labor

clues.clue: Commodity fetishism: attributing value to objects instead of their production labor
examples: markets cause people to fetishize the value of commodities
commodity fetishism, a form of reification

clues.clue: Base-superstructure model: economic base determines political/ideological superstructure
examples: argued that the ideological superstructure is an expression of the economic base
borrowed this thinker's ideas of the base and superstructure

clues.clue: Reserve army of labor: unemployed depress wages
examples: described the unemployed as a 'reserve army of labor' whose function was to depress workers' wages

clues.clue: Joseph Schumpeter popularized 'creative destruction' based on Marx
examples: Joseph Schumpeter popularized the notion of 'creative destruction' based on the writings of this thinker

clues.clue: Called Bentham a leather-tongued 'arch-philistine'
examples: called Bentham a leather-tongued 'arch-philistine'

clues.clue: G.A. Cohen's Karl Marx's Theory of History: Primacy Thesis and Development Thesis; founded September Group / analytical Marxism
examples: A 1978 book about this philosopher divides his core idea into a 'Primacy Thesis' and a 'Development Thesis'
that book about this thinker led to the creation of the September Group

works.name: Historical Materialism and Alienation
works.description: Marx's theory of historical materialism argues that economic processes and class conflict drive history. Societies progress through stages beginning with primitive accumulation and including capitalism. He argued history repeats itself, 'first as tragedy, then as farce' (from The Eighteenth Brumaire of Louis Napoleon). His concept of alienation holds that workers become estranged from their 'species-being' and the products of their labor through 'objectification.' The 1844 manuscripts introduced alienation and were analyzed by Althusser as the 'Young Marx.'

clues.clue: Historical materialism: economic base drives history; class conflict; societies progress through stages
examples: defended historical materialism in Das Kapital
human societies progress over six stages, beginning with primitive accumulation

clues.clue: 'First as tragedy, then as farce' (from Eighteenth Brumaire of Louis Napoleon)
examples: coined the phrase 'first as tragedy, then as farce'
this thinker coined the phrase 'first as tragedy, then as farce'

clues.clue: Alienation: workers estranged from species-being and products of labor through objectification
examples: social stratification leads agents to become estranged from their 'species-being'
by acting on the physical world, individuals experience 'objectification'

clues.clue: 1844 manuscripts introduced alienation; Althusser identified 'epistemological break' between Young and Mature Marx
examples: examines a series of 1844 manuscripts that introduced his concept of 'alienation'
one scholar of this thinker identified an 'epistemological break' between his early and mature works

clues.clue: Architect vs. bee: architect 'raises structure in imagination before erecting it in reality'
examples: illustrated one concept by contrasting the architect, who 'raises his structure in imagination before he erects it in reality,' with the bee

clues.clue: Karl Popper: reliance on historicism paved way for totalitarianism (The Open Society and Its Enemies)
examples: Karl Popper argues that this thinker's reliance on historicism paved the way for totalitarian rule

works.name: Theses on Feuerbach and The German Ideology
works.description: Marx wrote 11 short Theses on Feuerbach, the last of which states 'Philosophers have only interpreted the world; the point is to change it.' They criticize Feuerbach for restricting human essence to the individual rather than seeing it as a product of social relations, and for privileging theoretical activity. The Theses served as an outline for The German Ideology, co-written with Engels, which attacks Bruno Bauer, Feuerbach, and Max Stirner. The German Ideology contains the 'hunting in the morning, fishing in the afternoon' passage and calls Stirner a 'saint.'

clues.clue: 'Philosophers have only interpreted the world; the point is to change it' (11th thesis on Feuerbach)
examples: wrote that 'philosophers have only interpreted the world' but 'the point ... is to change it'
Philosophers have only interpreted the world... the point, however, is to change it

clues.clue: Criticized Feuerbach for conceiving practice 'only in its dirty Jewish manifestation' and restricting human essence to the individual
examples: accused the other of privileging theoretical activity and conceiving practice 'only in its dirty Jewish manifestation'
critiqued this philosopher for restricting human essence to the individual

clues.clue: German Ideology attacks Bauer, Feuerbach, and Stirner; contains 'hunt in the morning, fish in the afternoon' passage
examples: attacked philosophers like Bruno Bauer and Ludwig Feuerbach
'hunt in the morning, fish in the afternoon, rear cattle in the evening'

clues.clue: Cherry tree argument: ridiculed Feuerbach for finding 'sensuous certainty' in a cherry tree without noting it was transplanted
examples: ridiculed the other for claiming that we can find 'sensuous certainty' in a cherry tree but ignoring the fact that cherry trees only grow in his country because they have been transplanted

works.name: The Communist Manifesto
works.description: The Communist Manifesto (1848), co-written with Friedrich Engels, opens with 'a spectre is haunting Europe' and ends with 'workers of the world, unite.' It emphasizes class struggle. The phrase 'from each according to his ability, to each according to his need' comes from the Critique of the Gotha Program.

clues.clue: 'A spectre is haunting Europe'; 'workers of the world, unite'; published as pamphlet in 1848
examples: wrote that 'a spectre is haunting Europe'
ends with the declaration 'workers of the world, unite'

clues.clue: 'From each according to his ability, to each according to his need' (Critique of the Gotha Program)
examples: often used the slogan 'from each according to his ability, to each according to his need'

clues.clue: 'Religion is the opiate of the masses'
examples: claimed that 'religion is the opiate of the masses'

works.name: Althusser and Derrida (Reception)
works.description: Louis Althusser led 'structural' Marxism, co-writing Reading Capital and publishing For Marx (which identified an 'epistemological break'). He borrowed base/superstructure and relations of production, and wrote on Ideological State Apparatuses. Jacques Derrida's Specters of Marx coined the term 'hauntology' and argued Marx's ideas still haunt Western society. Mark Fisher's Capitalist Realism and Ghosts of My Life extended hauntology. Slavoj Zizek's Sublime Object of Ideology combined Marx and Freud. Gyorgy Lukacs founded Western Marxism (reification, History and Class Consciousness). Kojin Karatani's Transcritique examined Kant and Marx together.

clues.clue: Althusser: 'epistemological break' between Young and Mature; Reading Capital; structural Marxism; ISAs
examples: A 'symptomatic reading' of this thinker's work by Louis Althusser identified a 'break' between his early and late writings
Louis Althusser discussed the ideological state apparatus

clues.clue: Derrida's Specters of Marx coined 'hauntology'; argues Marx's ideas haunt Western society
examples: A book partly titled for this thinker by Jacques Derrida coined the term 'hauntology'
argued that their ideas are still haunting Western society

clues.clue: Mark Fisher: Capitalist Realism and Ghosts of My Life extended hauntology
examples: Fisher's book Capitalist Realism argues that this thinker's theories have been wrongly made to appear unrealistic

clues.clue: Lukacs: reification, History and Class Consciousness; applied Marx to literary criticism
examples: This thinker, who developed the theory of reification
His theories were applied to literary criticism by Gyorgy Lukacs

clues.clue: Zizek's Sublime Object of Ideology combines Marx with psychoanalysis
examples: A tradition focused on these two thinkers is analyzed in Slavoj Zizek's The Sublime Object of Ideology

clues.clue: Althusser: For Marx borrows Freudian 'overdetermination'
examples: A book titled for this thinker borrows a Freudian term for multiple lines of causation

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Marx is a German philosopher and economist, author of Das Kapital and The Communist Manifesto (with Engels). He was a Young Hegelian and his dialectics built on Hegel, twisting it from spiritual to economic processes. Peter Singer wrote the Very Short Introduction on Marx. The Grundrisse is another major text. The Critique of the Gotha Program criticizes legal equality as inherently unequal. He also wrote Scorpion and Felix (a novel) and The Eighteenth Brumaire of Louis Napoleon.

clues.clue: German philosopher/economist; author of Das Kapital
examples: name this German author of Das Kapital
this author of the Grundrisse

clues.clue: Young Hegelian; twisted Hegel's dialectic from spiritual to economic
examples: agreed with Hegel's notion that history is a dialectical process but twisted it from a spiritual process to an economic one

clues.clue: Peter Singer wrote the Very Short Introduction on Marx
examples: Peter Singer wrote the entry on this thinker from Oxford's Very Short Introductions series

clues.clue: Critique of the Gotha Program: legal equality is inherently unequal
examples: claimed that legal equality is inherently unequal

clues.clue: Scorpion and Felix (novel): 'Every giant presupposes a dwarf'
examples: this philosopher's novel Scorpion and Felix

comprehensive_summary:
Karl Marx is one of the most frequently clued philosophers in quizbowl, with 10 tossups and 20 bonuses. His universal giveaway is as the German philosopher/economist who wrote Das Kapital and The Communist Manifesto with Friedrich Engels.

Das Kapital (subtitled 'A Critique of Political Economy') distinguishes between use-value and exchange-value (a measure of 'congealed labour-time'), theorizes surplus value from labor exploitation, introduces commodity fetishism, and presents the base-superstructure model. The reserve army of labor depresses wages. G.A. Cohen's Karl Marx's Theory of History founded analytical Marxism / the September Group with its 'Primacy Thesis' and 'Development Thesis.'

Historical materialism argues economic processes and class conflict drive history through progressive stages. 'First as tragedy, then as farce' comes from The Eighteenth Brumaire of Louis Napoleon. His theory of alienation (from the 1844 manuscripts) holds that workers become estranged from their 'species-being' through objectification. Althusser identified an 'epistemological break' between the Young and Mature Marx.

The 11 Theses on Feuerbach culminate in 'Philosophers have only interpreted the world; the point is to change it.' The German Ideology (with Engels) attacks Bauer, Feuerbach, and Stirner, containing the 'hunt in the morning' passage. The Communist Manifesto opens with 'a spectre is haunting Europe' and ends with 'workers of the world, unite.' The Critique of the Gotha Program includes 'from each according to his ability, to each according to his need.'

Key interpreters: Althusser (structural Marxism, Reading Capital, ISAs, For Marx); Derrida (Specters of Marx, hauntology); Mark Fisher (Capitalist Realism, hauntology); Lukacs (reification, History and Class Consciousness); Zizek (Sublime Object of Ideology). Marx was a Young Hegelian who built on Hegel's dialectic, twisting it from spiritual to economic processes.

Philosopher: argues the economic base determines the political/ideological superstructure of society
Concept: the attribution of value to objects instead of their production labor in a capitalist society
Philosopher: G.A. Cohen's 1978 book divided this thinker's theory of history into 'Primacy' and 'Development' theses, founding the September Group / analytical Marxism
Philosopher: argued history repeats itself 'first as tragedy, then as farce' in The Eighteenth Brumaire of Louis Napoleon
Philosopher: workers become estranged from their 'species-being' and products of labor through 'objectification'; 1844 manuscripts introduced this concept of alienation
Philosopher: Louis Althusser identified an 'epistemological break' between the 'Young' and 'Mature' works of this thinker in For [him]
Philosopher: Jacques Derrida coined 'hauntology' in Specters of [this thinker], arguing his ideas still haunt Western society
Philosopher: wrote 'Philosophers have only interpreted the world; the point is to change it' in the 11th of his Theses on Feuerbach
